title: Discounted Cash Flow: A company is evaluating two projects with the following cash...
summary: Compare two projects using discounted cash flow analysis to identify the preferred investment.
prompt: A company is evaluating two projects with the following cash flows: Project A: Year 1: $50,000; Year 2: $60,000; Project B: Year 1: $70,000; Year 2: $40,000. If the company uses a discount rate of 6%, which project has a higher NPV?
solution: Step 1: Calculate NPV for Project A: NPV = $50,000 / (1 + 0.06) + $60,000 / (1 + 0.06)^2 = $47,169.81 + $53,490.97 = $100,660.78. Step 2: Calculate NPV for Project B: NPV = $70,000 / (1 + 0.06) + $40,000 / (1 + 0.06)^2 = $66,037.74 + $35,446.31 = $101,484.05. Step 3: Compare NPVs: Project B has a higher NPV of $101,484.05 compared to Project A's $100,660.78.
